treasure.summary: Accumulated plunder of ~50 prizes: coin, gold dust, Akan gold jewelry, ivory, indigo
treasure.recovered: 200,000+ artifacts incl. the inscribed ship's bell, coins, gold Akan jewelry; main coin mass still sought

whydah.salvage: Cyprian Southack, sent by Massachusetts, salvaged almost nothing in 1717; Barry Clifford found her in 1984 and excavation continues.
whydah.condition: Artifacts and concretions scattered in shifting sand; no coherent hull.
whydah.epitaph: Here foundered the Whydah of Black Sam Bellamy, with the wealth of fifty prizes in her hold; of seven-score souls but two came ashore alive.
manifest: ~180 sacks of coin, fifty pounds apiece (trial testimony of survivors)
Gold dust and Akan gold jewelry
Elephants' teeth (ivory)
Indigo and Jesuit's bark (quinine)
28 mounted cannon

treasure.summary: Stripped by Blackbeard before the grounding; only traces of gold dust remained
treasure.recovered: 31 cannon, anchors and hundreds of thousands of artifacts, incl. gold dust grains, medical instruments and a bell cast 1705

queen-annes-revenge.salvage: No contemporary salvage; located 1996 by Intersal, excavated by North Carolina's QAR Project since 1997.
queen-annes-revenge.condition: Ballast mound with concreted cannon and anchors; little hull structure survives.
queen-annes-revenge.epitaph: Here Black Beard ran his great ship upon the bar, and cheated his own company of their shares.
manifest: 31 cast-iron cannon of French, English and Swedish founding
Gold dust scattered through the ballast
Medical instruments, incl. a urethral syringe — relics of the Charleston ransom of 1718
Lead sounding weights, pewter and glassware
Bell cast 1705, likely Spanish

treasure.summary: A storeship's cargo — hides, chocolate, incense — with private silver chests, salvaged in period
treasure.recovered: Spanish salvors emptied her 1715–16; the modern preserve holds ballast and hull, not treasure

urca-de-lima.salvage: Spanish salvage camps worked her 1715–16; Henry Jennings raided the camp at Palmar de Ays in 1716; Florida preserve since 1987.
urca-de-lima.condition: Ballast and lower hull remains with replica cannon in ~3 m off Fort Pierce.
urca-de-lima.epitaph: Here the storm set down the Urca de Lima, alone of all the flota to keep her bones together.
manifest: Cowhides and tanned leather
Chocolate (cacao), vanilla and sassafras
Incense
Private chests of silver specie (salvaged 1715–16)

treasure.summary: The royal consignment of the 1715 flota: ~1,300 chests of silver pesos and Mexican gold
treasure.period_value: part of ~14 million pesos lost with the 1715 fleet
treasure.recovered: Spain salvaged much of the registered silver 1715–18; Real Eight Co. raised thousands of coins from 1961; coins still wash ashore after storms

nuestra-senora-regla.salvage: Spanish salvage camps 1715–18; Kip Wagner's Real Eight Co. from 1961 founded modern Treasure Coast salvage.
nuestra-senora-regla.condition: Broken and dispersed off Sebastian Inlet; ballast scatter and coin trails in shallow sand.
nuestra-senora-regla.epitaph: Here broke the capitana of the flota in minutes, and General Ubilla and two hundred more went down with the King's silver.
manifest: ~1,300 chests of silver pesos
Mexican gold coin and worked plate
New Spain produce: cochineal, indigo, hides
Passengers' private fortunes, registered and not

treasure.summary: The treasure of the Portobelo fair: gold and silver coin and ingots, Peruvian emeralds
treasure.recovered: Nothing raised; cannon, coin and porcelain photographed in situ by Colombian missions

san-jose.salvage: Never salvaged; located 2015 by a REMUS 6000 AUV (WHOI with the Colombian Navy); ownership contested by Colombia, Spain and Sea Search Armada.
san-jose.condition: Broken hull and wide debris field at ~600 m; bronze cannon with dolphin handles visible on the seabed.
san-jose.epitaph: Here blew up the great San José in action with Wager, and six hundred men and the King of Spain's treasure went down in a breath.
manifest: Gold and silver coin of the Lima mint
Silver ingots and worked plate
Emeralds of the Muzo mines
Chinese export porcelain (seen in the debris field)

treasure.summary: No treasure — stripped, burned and scuttled at Kidd's own order
treasure.recovered: Ballast stones and timber fragments; the 2015 'silver ingot' proved to be lead

adventure-galley.salvage: Stripped by Kidd's company in 1698; Clifford expeditions 1999–2015; a 2015 UNESCO mission disputed the hull identification.
adventure-galley.condition: Ballast pile and scattered timbers in harbour silt at Île Sainte-Marie; identification debated.
adventure-galley.epitaph: Here Captain Kidd burned his leaking Adventure, and with her every hope of an honest accounting.
manifest: Ballast stones and sheathing only
34 guns when fitted out in 1695, removed before scuttling
Her plunder carried away in the Quedagh Merchant

treasure.summary: Sweepings of the Indian Ocean trade: Kangxi porcelain, gold dust, cowries
treasure.recovered: Thousands of porcelain sherds, gold dust, glass and cowries excavated in the 2000s

fiery-dragon.salvage: Excavated in the 2000s by the Center for Historic Shipwreck Preservation with Malagasy partners.
fiery-dragon.condition: Burned lower hull and artifact scatter under the silt of the pirate anchorage.
fiery-dragon.epitaph: Here burned the Fiery Dragon at her moorings, her China-ware strewn upon the harbour floor.
manifest: Chinese Kangxi-era export porcelain
Gold dust
Cowrie shells, the money of the Guinea trade
Glassware and goods from Red Sea prizes

treasure.summary: Red Sea plunder; the crew carried much of the loot ashore with them
treasure.recovered: Coins (Arabian and Indian among them), pewter and cannon recovered from 1979

speaker.salvage: The pirates salvaged their own loot in 1702; found 1979 by Jacques Dumas, excavated by Patrick Lizé — the first pirate wreck ever excavated.
speaker.condition: Cannon and ballast scattered on the Grand Port reef.
speaker.epitaph: Here the Speaker struck the reef at Grand Port, and her pirates waded ashore richer than their hosts.
manifest: Coin of Arabia and India from Red Sea prizes
Pewter ware
Cannon and small arms
The remainder carried ashore by the crew

treasure.summary: No treasure: homeward sugar and logwood — and the iron evidence of the slave trade
treasure.recovered: Her bell ('THE HENRIETTA MARIE 1699'), more than 80 pairs of shackles, cannon, ivory and pewter

henrietta-marie.salvage: No contemporary salvage; found 1972 by Mel Fisher's divers hunting the Atocha, identified by her bell in 1983; NABS memorial plaque laid on the site 1993.
henrietta-marie.condition: Ballast, cannon and hull fragments on New Ground Reef.
henrietta-marie.epitaph: Here sank the slaver Henrietta Marie, her irons empty, her reckoning eternal.
manifest: Sugar and logwood from Jamaica
Cotton
More than 80 pairs of iron shackles (bilboes)
Elephant ivory from the Guinea coast
Ship's bell cast 1699

treasure.summary: 16,000 pesos of registered silver and Chinese porcelain, mostly fished up by Spanish divers
treasure.recovered: Spanish salvors recovered most of the silver 1733–38; the preserve retains ballast and replica cannon

san-pedro-1733.salvage: Spanish salvage camps 1733–38 recovered more silver than the manifests admitted; Florida preserve since 1989.
san-pedro-1733.condition: Ballast mound with replica cannon and anchor in Hawk Channel sand and seagrass.
san-pedro-1733.epitaph: Here the San Pedro settled in Hawk Channel, her silver fished up almost to the last peso.
manifest: 16,000 pesos in registered silver
Chinese porcelain, come by way of Manila and Veracruz
Tanned hides and dyewoods
Contraband silver beyond the registry, betrayed by the salvage tallies

treasure.summary: Three chests of coin for Batavia — gold ducats and silver ducatons
treasure.recovered: Some 2,000 gold ducats, silver coin, wine bottles and a sealed mailbag of legible letters (1981–1990s)

vliegent-hart.salvage: VOC attempts in 1735 failed; located 1981 by a Rex Cowan–Dutch partnership and excavated through the 1990s.
vliegent-hart.condition: Broken and buried in mobile sand off the Deurloo channel.
vliegent-hart.epitaph: Here went down the Flying Hart with all her people, in sight of the steeples of Vlissingen.
manifest: Three chests of coin: gold ducats and silver ducatons
Wine and beer in bottles and casks
Ironwork and stores for Batavia
A mailbag of private letters, recovered legible after 250 years

treasure.summary: Fleet pay chests and the admiral's plate
treasure.recovered: Coins and cannon raised from 1967; finds dispersed at auction

hms-association.salvage: Relocated 1967 by Royal Navy divers; commercial salvage scattered her coin and guns through the auction rooms.
hms-association.condition: Wreckage strewn through gullies off the Gilstone Ledge; nothing coherent.
hms-association.epitaph: Here the rocks of Scilly took the Association and her admiral, for want of a longitude.
manifest: Pay chests of the Mediterranean fleet
Admiral Shovell's plate and effects
90 guns of a second rate
Homeward Mediterranean goods

treasure.summary: Kidd's great prize, looted by his trustees before they burned her
treasure.period_value: cargo sold off India valued near £50,000 when taken (disputed)
treasure.recovered: 26 cannon and anchors mapped in situ; nothing removed — a living museum preserve

quedagh-merchant.salvage: Looted and burned by Kidd's trustees in 1699; found 2007 by Indiana University divers; Dominican living museum since 2011.
quedagh-merchant.condition: Pile of cannon and anchors on coral rubble in three metres of water off Isla Catalina.
quedagh-merchant.epitaph: Here Kidd's great prize was rifled and burnt by the very men he trusted to keep her.
manifest: Bengal silk and muslin (sold off before the burning)
Calico and chintz
Sugar and opium
Iron and saltpetre

treasure.summary: Quicksilver for the silver mines of Mexico, in iron-bound chests
treasure.recovered: Thousands of artifacts — mercury, wine bottles, pottery — raised 1976–77, now in the Museo de las Casas Reales

guadalupe-1724.salvage: Spain recovered survivors and some cargo 1724–25; Tracy Bowden's Caribe Salvage excavated her 1976–77 under Dominican contract.
guadalupe-1724.condition: Lower hull in the sand of Samaná Bay shallows.
guadalupe-1724.epitaph: Here the Guadalupe drove ashore with the King's quicksilver, that the mines of Mexico might go hungry.
manifest: Quicksilver (144 tons between her and the Tolosa)
Wine and brandy in onion-bottles
Ironwork and tools
Passengers' chests and effects

treasure.summary: Mercury, passengers' jewels and pearls, Puebla pottery
treasure.recovered: Mercury, gold jewelry, pearls and Puebla majolica raised 1976–77

tolosa-1724.salvage: Excavated 1976–77 with the Guadalupe by Caribe Salvage; finds in Santo Domingo.
tolosa-1724.condition: Broken on a reef deeper in Samaná Bay; artifact-rich lower works in sand.
tolosa-1724.epitaph: Here the Tolosa broke on Samaná's reef; eight men lived a month in her mizzen-top on rain and chocolate.
manifest: Quicksilver in skins, boxed in chests
Gold jewelry and pearls of her passengers
Puebla majolica pottery
Wine bottles by the thousand

treasure.summary: ~1,500 silver bars and three chests of coin, outward for Batavia
treasure.recovered: Lethbridge raised the greater part of the silver 1725–27 from his diving barrel; Sténuit recovered remaining bars and coin in 1974

slot-ter-hooge.salvage: John Lethbridge's famous wooden 'diving engine' worked her for the VOC 1725–27; Robert Sténuit relocated and finished the job in 1974.
slot-ter-hooge.condition: Scattered remains under sand and rock off Porto Santo, Madeira.
slot-ter-hooge.epitaph: Here sank the Slot ter Hooge upon Porto Santo, whence the diver Lethbridge drew her silver up in his barrel.
manifest: ~1,500 silver bars
Three chests of silver coin
Trade goods and stores for the Indies

treasure.summary: 227,000 guilders in coin for the Indies trade
treasure.recovered: Thousands of silver ducatons and gold ducats raised 1965–68; displayed in Lerwick

de-liefde.salvage: Early fishing of guns by Shetlanders; systematic salvage 1965–68 by a consortium including Navy divers.
de-liefde.condition: Scattered through kelp gullies off the Out Skerries.
de-liefde.epitaph: Here the Liefde struck the Out Skerries by night; one man alone was cast living on the rocks.
manifest: 227,000 guilders in silver ducatons and gold ducats
Trade goods for Batavia
Ship's guns and stores

treasure.summary: Nineteen chests of new coin — some 57,000 gold and silver pieces
treasure.recovered: ~57,000 coins — the 'Runde treasure' — raised 1972 and shared between Norway and the Netherlands

akerendam.salvage: No period salvage; found by sport divers off Runde in 1972 — the largest treasure ever recovered in Norway.
akerendam.condition: Scattered at the foot of the Runde skerries; guns and anchors remain.
akerendam.epitaph: Here perished the Akerendam on her maiden voyage, all hands, her gold beneath the skerries of Runde.
manifest: Nineteen chests of newly minted coin: gold ducats and silver
Stores and goods for the outward voyage
Cannon and anchors, still on the bottom

treasure.summary: ~5 million pesos in silver, gold and emeralds, much of it contraband
treasure.recovered: Salvaged piecemeal for a century by Spanish divers and Bahamian 'wrackers'; modern finds incl. gold chains and gemstones (Allen Exploration, Bahamas Maritime Museum)

maravillas.salvage: Spanish expeditions from 1656, then English and freelance wrackers for eighty years; Marx 1972; Marex 1980s–90s; Allen Exploration from 2020.
maravillas.condition: Scattered for miles along Little Bahama Bank; no hull survives.
maravillas.epitaph: Here the Maravillas was rammed and lost with five millions; eighty years of wrackers have fished her bones.
manifest: Registered silver of the 1656 fleet, with contraband above it
Gold chains and worked plate
Colombian emeralds and amethysts
Bronze cannon, long since fished up

treasure.summary: Registered silver, gold and emeralds of the 1622 Tierra Firme fleet
treasure.recovered: Mel Fisher's 1985 motherlode: silver ingots, ~thousands of coins, gold bars and emeralds; the sterncastle treasure is still missing

atocha.salvage: Spanish salvors marked but never reached her, 1622–23 (her consort Margarita was part-salvaged by Melián's diving bell from 1626); Fisher's crews searched 1969–85.
atocha.condition: Scattered along a miles-long trail in the Marquesas; lower hull timbers found beneath the sand.
atocha.epitaph: Here lies the Atocha, the great unfinished salvage of the Indies, her silver told of in every Havana tavern.
manifest: 901 silver ingots and ~255,000 silver coins (registry of 1622)
161 gold bars and discs
Raw emeralds of the Muzo mines
Copper ingots, indigo and tobacco

treasure.summary: Twelve chests of silver coin and rich trade goods for the Indies
treasure.recovered: Pelsaert's salvors raised ten of twelve coin chests in 1629; hull timbers, the stone portico and victims' skeletons excavated from the 1970s

batavia-1629.salvage: Pelsaert returned in the Sardam, salvaged the chests and hanged the mutineers on the spot, 1629; Western Australian Museum excavation from the 1970s.
batavia-1629.condition: Articulated stern hull section raised and displayed at Fremantle; ballast and guns remain on Morning Reef.
batavia-1629.epitaph: Here struck the Batavia, and what men did after upon these isles is not fit to be told at length.
manifest: Twelve chests of silver rijksdaalders (ten recovered 1629)
Antiquities and jewels for the Mughal trade, incl. a great agate cameo
A pre-cut sandstone portico for the gate of Batavia castle
Trade silver and cloth

treasure.summary: Colonial pay chests: Spanish-American, Massachusetts and New York silver
treasure.recovered: Coins recovered after 1968 surfaced at auction through the 1990s — a unique sample of the cash that circulated in the colonies

hms-feversham.salvage: Located 1968 by Alex Storm after his Chameau success; partial recovery, finds dispersed at auction.
hms-feversham.condition: Broken on the shoals of Scatarie Island; artifacts scattered.
hms-feversham.epitaph: Here the Feversham and her convoy broke on Scatarie, the soldiers' pay strewn among the rocks.
manifest: Massachusetts pine-tree shillings
New York and Spanish-American silver coin
Guns and stores of the Quebec expedition

treasure.summary: None of note — Bonnet's last cruise had earned only small river prizes
treasure.recovered: Nothing; no confirmed hull has been found

royal-james.salvage: None; South Carolina archaeologists have surveyed the Cape Fear engagement area without a confirmed wreck.
royal-james.condition: Unlocated; the river's shifting sandbars cover any remains of the battle.
royal-james.epitaph: Here the gentleman pirate fought Colonel Rhett five hours upon the sands, and lost his ship, and after, his neck.
manifest: Ten guns of small calibre
Stores and small plunder of a river cruise
Battle debris of the five-hour fight on the bars

treasure.summary: Registered silver of the flota, recovered almost wholly by Spanish divers — with contraband besides
treasure.recovered: Spain recovered more silver than the registry admitted, 1733–34; Art McKee raised cannon, anchors and coin from 1938

el-rubi-capitana-1733.salvage: Spanish salvage camps 1733–34, the wrecks then burned to the waterline; Art McKee's pioneering hard-hat salvage from 1938.
el-rubi-capitana-1733.condition: Burned to the waterline in 1733; ballast mound and scattered fittings off Plantation Key.
el-rubi-capitana-1733.epitaph: Here the Capitana of the flota was driven on the reef and burned, her silver counted twice over.
manifest: Registered silver specie and bullion (salvaged 1733–34)
Cochineal, indigo and dyewoods
New Spain produce: hides, vanilla, chocolate
Unregistered contraband, betrayed by the salvage tallies

treasure.summary: Chests of new-minted 1711 Middelburg silver — schellingen and double stuivers
treasure.recovered: A 'carpet of silver' coins recovered from the surf zone by Western Australian Museum expeditions

zuytdorp.salvage: None in period — the VOC never learned her fate; wreckage found by stockman Tom Pepper 1927, identified 1958, museum work from the 1970s.
zuytdorp.condition: Ground to pieces in the surf at the cliff foot; coin carpet, cannon and fittings.
zuytdorp.epitaph: Here the Zuytdorp drove against the iron cliffs of the South Land, and no word of her ever came to Batavia.
manifest: Chests of silver schellingen and double stuivers, Middelburg mint, 1711
VOC trade goods and outward stores
Cannon and anchors at the cliff base

treasure.summary: Ten chests of coin, 315,836 guilders — nearly all carried away to Batavia by her own castaways
treasure.recovered: Most specie saved by the survivors themselves in the Sloepie, 1728; WA Museum recovered artifacts 1976–81

zeewijk.salvage: Self-salvage by the castaways 1727–28; systematic Western Australian Museum excavation 1976–81.
zeewijk.condition: Guns, anchors and scatter on Half Moon Reef; survivor camp remains on Gun Island.
zeewijk.epitaph: Here the Zeewijk struck, and her men built from her bones the first ship ever made in the South Land.
manifest: Ten chests of silver coin (315,836 guilders)
Building materials and trade goods for Batavia
Guns and anchors, still on the reef
Camp debris on Gun Island: bottles, clay pipes, tools

treasure.summary: The year's funds for New France in gold louis d'or and silver écus
treasure.recovered: Alex Storm's team raised the coin pocket in 1965; the treasure was auctioned in 1971

le-chameau.salvage: French parties in 1725 recovered bodies and flotsam only; Storm, MacEachern and MacLeod found the pay after a systematic search, 1965.
le-chameau.condition: Wreckage strewn from Chameau Rock across the seabed; nothing coherent.
le-chameau.epitaph: Here the Chameau struck in the dark with all the money of Canada, and not one soul came ashore alive.
manifest: Gold louis d'or and silver écus (~290,000 livres)
Stores and goods for Quebec and Louisbourg
Effects of the incoming intendant and officials of New France

treasure.summary: No treasure — her extraordinary preservation is the prize
treasure.recovered: Guns, the ship's copper kettle and navigation gear recovered as the sands shift; protected wreck

hms-stirling-castle.salvage: None in period; discovered 1979 when the Goodwins uncovered her; protected and monitored since 1980.
hms-stirling-castle.condition: Remarkably intact hull, periodically exposed and reburied by the Goodwin Sands.
hms-stirling-castle.epitaph: Here the Great Storm swallowed the Stirling Castle into the Goodwins, which keep her yet, wondrous whole.
manifest: 70 guns of a third rate
The ship's copper galley kettle
Surgeon's and navigator's instruments
Stores of a ship of the line

treasure.summary: By strong documentary evidence, a great coin shipment to buy the Duke of Savoy's alliance
treasure.recovered: Nothing; Odyssey Marine's 2002 recovery agreement stalled in British–Spanish diplomacy

hms-sussex.salvage: Impossible in period at such depth; Odyssey Marine surveyed a deep-water candidate site in 2001.
hms-sussex.condition: Deep-water cannon mound ~800 m down in the Gibraltar approaches; identification unconfirmed.
hms-sussex.epitaph: Here the levanter overset the Sussex with the King's gold for Savoy; the sea keeps both the secret and the subsidy.
manifest: A reputed ~10 tons of coin for the Savoy subsidy (National Archives correspondence)
80 guns of a third rate
Stores of the Mediterranean fleet

treasure.summary: No treasure: guns and stores, her crew already dead or dying of disease
treasure.recovered: Cannon raised 1938–39 for Florida collections; anchors and fittings

hms-winchester.salvage: Charles Brookfield's hard-hat salvage of 1938–39, recounted in National Geographic.
hms-winchester.condition: Ballast and cannon scatter on Carysfort Reef.
hms-winchester.epitaph: Here drove the Winchester upon Carysfort Reef, her people too few and too sick to wear ship.
manifest: 60 guns of a fourth rate
Stores depleted by a dying crew
Surgeon's stores exhausted by scurvy and fever

treasure.summary: Eight chests of silver coin, 78,600 guilders, for the Batavia trade
treasure.recovered: Coins, elephant tusks, ballast and fittings recovered after 1963 by the Western Australian Museum

vergulde-draeck.salvage: VOC rescue voyages 1656–58 found no survivors and lost more men; WA Museum excavation from 1972.
vergulde-draeck.condition: Broken on an inshore reef; concreted coin masses and tusks recovered.
vergulde-draeck.epitaph: Here the Gilt Dragon spilled her silver on the reefs of the South Land, and her people walked into silence.
manifest: Eight chests of silver coin (78,600 guilders)
Elephant tusks
Trade goods for Batavia
Ballast bricks

treasure.summary: Nothing — stripped for careening before the battle that destroyed her
treasure.recovered: Site identified 2015 by the Chatterton–Mattera search; artifacts surveyed, little raised

golden-fleece.salvage: Burned in the two-day action of June 1686; identified 2015, the hunt recounted in Kurson's 'Pirate Hunters'.
golden-fleece.condition: Burned remains under sand in a careening cove of Samaná Bay.
golden-fleece.epitaph: Here Bannister careened the Golden Fleece and fought two of the King's frigates from the shore — then walked away.
manifest: Careening gear and shore-battery guns
Stripped stores — her plunder was landed before the fight
Charred hull timbers
